category_sources: source_facts.description says this is an I/O abstraction library for structured file formats; official GSF reference manual describes the same purpose: https://gnome.pages.gitlab.gnome.org/libgsf/api/
display_name_sources: source_facts.name is libgsf; official GNOME GitLab project path and API docs use libgsf/GSF: https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libgsf
docs_sources: Official GNOME Pages GSF Reference Manual: https://gnome.pages.gitlab.gnome.org/libgsf/api/
repo_sources: source_facts.repo is empty; source_facts.homepage points to the official GNOME GitLab project: https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libgsf
tags_sources: source_facts.description names structured file formats; official GSF reference manual covers structured files, streams, ZIP, compression, metadata, and OASIS Open Document: https://gnome.pages.gitlab.gnome.org/libgsf/api/
